[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByBlockType() uses reference equality ('block == BlockType.LATEST') rather than value equality, and since BlockType is a record, two BlockType instances with the same name and number are NOT the same reference unless they are the static constants, causing a BlockType created by BlockType.of('latest') to correctly match LATEST but a BlockType created programmatically with new BlockType('latest', Long.MAX_VALUE) to fall through to findByIndex(Long.MAX_VALUE), returning empty and disrupting staking reward context resolution?
